item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I updated the wikiwalk proposal with that suggestion.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
I feel putting the link in the redaction itself kind of spoils the purpose of a redaction in the first place.

I'm fine with the link to 003, but I would rather have a sentence added at the end of the paragraph that says something like this:

Due to SCP-2579-1's similar material composition to SCP-003, research has begun to discover possible connections.

Or something to that affect. I'll let you play around with it a bit.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

I see room for a crosslink to SCP-003, due to the "anomalous computers" in the bear heads. Both involve circuitboards made out of organic materials that "grow".

Editing this:

SCP-2579-1 is a small device embedded in the brains of all adult instances of SCP-2579. The device naturally forms over time as instances grow. An instance of SCP-2579-1 has three primary components: a small solid-state storage drive, a wireless signal receiver, and the external cover. The cover is primarily made of a keratin-like substance. The storage drive and the wireless signal receiver appear to be made from bone, iron deposits, silicon, and [REDACTED]. SCP-2579-1 do not seem to impede SCP-2579 instances in any way.

To this:

SCP-2579-1 is a small device embedded in the brains of all adult instances of SCP-2579. The device naturally forms over time as instances grow. An instance of SCP-2579-1 has three primary components: a small solid-state storage drive, a wireless signal receiver, and the external cover. The cover is primarily made of a keratin-like substance. The storage drive and the wireless signal receiver appear to be made from bone, iron deposits, silicon, and [REDACTED]. SCP-2579-1 do not seem to impede SCP-2579 instances in any way. Due to SCP-2579-1's similar material composition to SCP-003, it is theorized that the two anomalies are related.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes! Gangnam style!! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh … So that's what the D-Class personnel do when they're not busy being fed through anomalous meat grinders and wood chippers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, looking back on this, I totally understand they're not developed enough. Once the 3000 contest is finished, I may come back to this and expand on it a bit. But I have a few other projects I want to finish first. I definitely do plan on adding more to it later though. Thanks for the feedback

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: THIS. I said on chat that the GOI feels like it should be pruned due to the fact that it added nothing and felt cliche. When I read this, I realized that it could be developed instead. Both of these options would add to the article, in my opinion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is funny, but very thin and ultimately doesn't grow its premise. The zoo angle is a good one along with the cybernetic inplants but they're not developed enough in tbe article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I guess my problem with the -1 is that it's too normal, strange as that may be. The most anomalous thing about it is that the bears grow it on their own. Otherwise it's just a weird piece of Gen+2 future-tech. Maybe I'm getting too jaded, but "dancing bears" becomes much less interesting after I know they have cybernetic implants with wifi and speakers.
I seem to be in the minority, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What about the tone should be changed? I would be grateful if you pointed to the sentences that seem off.

As for -1, I am well aware that I don't need to explain why the bears can dance, but with my own standards for writing I needed to. I also think a lot more people would be concerned with they "How" or the "Why" of this article if I didn't include it. By including it, readers can kind of understand why these bears exist. They're pieces of entertainment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I suggest you drop the -1 part entirely; here at the Foundation you are not required to explain why the bears can dance and play music.

I would disagree with this notion. It is purely up to the author if they want to explain the anomaly or not. Besides, the explanation we have already adds more questions to the table, making it rather effective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think -1 adds to the concept a lot. It's a good slice of body horror for an otherwise silly article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't say I like this. The basic idea is fine, but the tone isn't quite there.

I suggest you drop the -1 part entirely; here at the Foundation you are not required to explain why the bears can dance and play music.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Almost clicked out of the page when I saw "nae nae" in the description of the bear. I'm so glad I changed my mind because I had no idea that I needed this in my life. Probably one of my favorite SCPs so far. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first post on this discussion page has my inspiration for this horrible monstrosity. I got linked it by a friend.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How did you came up with this?

What decisions in your life lead to the creation of this?

I don't care

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In regards for there not being much going on in this article, other than the anomaly itself is mostly because of how little trust I have in my own ability to expand an article. I may eventually come back to this and add more, if I decide to flesh out Grant's Zoo or do something else.

As for the songs/dances, they're not really currently popular songs. But they were popular in the past few years and are either good dance songs or have dances specifically associated with them. I choose them particularly to give the reader an amusing picture, as a lot of people can visualize the songs or the dances and find that amusing. I'm probably going to be updating this from time to time, replacing the old songs/fad dances with newer ones as time goes on.

Thank you for the feedback though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This leaves me really torn. On the one hand I appreciate a funny article that's not a -J, it's competently written and does what it sets out to do without unnecessary fluff. The mental image of a breakdancing bear is pretty awesome. But at the same time there's just not enough there for me to upvote, I kept waiting for something else to come but it didn't. Also the references to specific songs were kinda 'meh', since (I assume) they are currently popular songs it feels weird to have them be part of the documentation of an everchanging anomaly.

All in all this is a novote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I second Nederbird. This is genuinely anomalous while not disturbing.

The devices in the skulls are slightly unsettling, offset by the effects they produce.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but a bear dancing while Shake It Off plays on the backround gives me traumatic flashbacks to Norm of The North.

1+

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Males will often attempt to perform dances associated with the songs being played.

And at this point I took off my glasses and channeled Captain Picard for a moment.

*throws +1 at dancing bear and walks off still facepalming*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This bear can't dance, so I had to show love for ones that can. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Bears…beats…"

Well, 2 outta 3 ain't bad, +1!

item.title: Re: Puns?
item.content:encoded: This article just bearly made it.

item.title: Puns?
item.content:encoded: I can bearly tolerate this. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-2579 + SCP-2875 = This, but with bears.

Capital!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A humorous SCP that not a joke SCP and still works really well. The anomalies come off as really bizarre, especially the composition of the device within the bears' heads *shivers*, while the mere mental image of the mental ritual is quite funny in its own right. Put a definite smile to my lips!

+1! It's nice to see all SCP's aren't dark, dreary, scary and ominous. The world's absurd, with its own natural amount of comedy. Great work, mate!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Bears. +1

This pretty much hits my interests: music and animals. It's silly, but not so silly I can't take it seriously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I initially downvoted it immediately because of the nae nae I saw in the caption. Turns out that this is actually a pretty good rendition of dancing bears. Still seems like it could be a joke scp, but it's decent enough fro me to change to a neutral vote. Good Job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 'Court' would be my suggestion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know 'woo' isn't the most clinical term, but I was having trouble coming up with a good word to use.

As for the succ.txt, I removed that after some input from people in chat. I may add an addenda later going over the bear's creation, but that won't be for awhile.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Jesus CHRIST.

Anywhos, let's talk about the article at hand.

A male instance of SCP-2579 performing the Nae Nae, a popular hip-hop dance, in an attempt to woo a female instance.

Don't think "woo" is a clinical term

these include a text file called "succ.txt,"

This is bearly1 passing off as "Silly enough to work" for me. Barely

Overall: Upvote because I didn't know I wanted this in my life.

I demand a cross-test with SCP-2875

Footnotes
1. The pun has been made, bitches

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is all but a cold-post. Inspired from this amazing video.

Image is from wikimedia commons, under the the CC2.0 license. LINK

E. This skip evidently took me one hour, from the initial drafting, to get to here. Why do I do this?
